title: Capital Steals Attention And Calls It Freedom
subtitle: A cleaned reading of Secret History #25
voice_policy: Direct argumentative prose derived from the lecture. Avoid repeated third-person framing. Do not impersonate first-person authorship except when quoting verified source material.
trace_policy: Paragraph-level transcript refs. Controversial historical claims remain attributed to this dated source.
dek: Capital is not a thing you own. It is a system that captures attention, manufactures anxiety, destroys stored security, and then moves somewhere else. Secret societies enter as the hidden trust machinery of capital that refuses place, nation, and responsibility.
thesis: The lecture is Jiang's theory of capital as spiritual extraction. Economics begins as a fight over grain, but grain is too concrete and peasants can stop once they have enough. Capital improves by becoming abstract: freedom replaces slavery, equity makes people work harder, money becomes God, debt creates anxiety, and wars or depressions burn down the granary. Once people become too exploited, the landlord class moves. That mobile landlord is transnational capital, and its practical form is the secret society: a ritual structure that lets elites trust one another, displace agency onto Satan or Saturn, train dependent initiates, pool money through banks, and pass the same merchant-oligarchy system from Venice to the Dutch Republic, England, and America.

opening.heading: Core Reading
opening.text: The first reversal is brutal because it sounds like economics and then stops being economics. Capital is not money, wealth, grain, gold, or a corporation. Capital is a mechanism for extracting human energy. Since consciousness is the universe, energy means attention and focus. A landlord does not merely want rent from the peasant; he wants the peasant's whole nervous system pointed at work. The history of capital is therefore the history of better attention capture: give people the illusion of freedom, make money so abstract that it becomes God, create debt and poverty so anxiety never stops, then periodically destroy stored wealth so nobody can rest. If the peasants finally revolt, capital moves. The ruling class has no homeland. It has only capital.

marks.text: Capital is a mechanism for extracting human energy
money becomes God
The ruling class has no homeland. It has only capital

beats.heading: Capital Is Attention Extraction
beats.summary: The lecture begins by replacing the economics definition of capital with a consciousness definition: capital extracts focus, and modern society gets better at making people focus through freedom, abstraction, anxiety, and destruction.

paragraphs.text: The peasant with grain is not yet maximally useful. Grain feeds a family, can be stored, and eventually lets a person stop. The landlord wants something more total: not merely rent, but concentration. The first improvement is freedom itself. Direct slavery is clumsy. Give the worker control, choice, a vote, or equity, and the worker begins to police his own attention.

marks.text: the worker begins to police his own attention

paragraphs.text: The second improvement is abstraction. Grain has a limit. Gold is harder, but still finite. Money is stronger because it can become the object of a whole life. It is both nothing and everything, and for that reason it can function religiously. Money is the God that requires no church because it already occupies the day.

marks.text: Money is the God that requires no church

paragraphs.text: The third improvement is anxiety. Debt means you never finish paying. Poverty means the middle class sees a pit beneath it. War and depression are not accidents inside capitalism; they are the burning of the granary. Stored security makes people lazy. Destroy it, and attention returns to work.

marks.text: the burning of the granary

beats.heading: Capital Learns To Move
beats.summary: When anxiety becomes revolt, capital escapes place. Jiang calls this transnational capital and identifies secret societies as its trust system and agency-displacement ritual.

paragraphs.text: Transnational capital is the landlord after he learns to leave. He goes to Dubai, Hong Kong, Singapore, anywhere taxes are lower, conflict is avoidable, and investment is better. He does not betray his country because country was never the object of loyalty. Capital was.

marks.text: country was never the object of loyalty. Capital was

paragraphs.text: Movement creates a practical problem: how do strangers trust one another? Jiang's answer is the lecture's hinge. Secret societies and transnational capital are essentially the same thing. A secret society is not decoration on the system; it is the social technology that lets capital coordinate across distance.

marks.text: Secret societies and transnational capital are essentially the same thing

paragraphs.text: The lender class also needs a religion different from peasant religion. Ordinary people can be told to be good, marry, pray, and live simply. The lender class must do terrible things while believing it is not personally doing them. The ritual answer is responsibility transfer: Satan told us, Saturn told us, order required it, paradise required it. Evil becomes obedience to a higher power.

marks.text: Evil becomes obedience to a higher power

beats.heading: Obedience Is Engineered
beats.summary: Authority, conformity, destroyed reality, sex, trauma, and dissociation become the machinery by which secret societies create loyal initiates, spies, and killers.

paragraphs.text: The Milgram experiment gives Jiang authority: people can hurt others when the command and responsibility come from elsewhere. The Asch experiment gives him conformity: a person can be told a line is a circle and eventually believe he saw a circle. Secret society power sits on those two levers.

marks.text: a person can be told a line is a circle and eventually believe he saw a circle

paragraphs.text: The darker part is initiation. Jiang's violent thought experiment is meant to show how destroyed reality produces dependency rather than clean vengeance. A person whose world collapses may cling to whoever supplies the next stable world. Degrees of secrecy are therefore degrees of dependency. The top knows, the initiates do not, and the trusted middle is made out of people willing to become slaves.

marks.text: Degrees of secrecy are therefore degrees of dependency

paragraphs.text: Sparta, gangs, cartels, child armies, and the Assassins become examples of the same formula: wound early, then arrive as home. Sex plus trauma plus hallucinogens creates the 'perfect slaves.' When a student asks why a victim would not remember the murderer, Jiang's answer is dissociation. The mind leaves; the handler stays in the back; only he knows the password.

marks.text: wound early, then arrive as home
only he knows the password

beats.heading: Merchant Oligarchy Gets More Abstract
beats.summary: Venice, the Dutch Republic, England, and America are treated as four versions of the same private-interest system, improving through banking, trade control, diplomacy, Protestantism, and the Enlightenment.

paragraphs.text: The historical sequence is Venice, the Dutch Republic, England, America. Each calls itself something respectable, often republic or democracy, but Jiang reads each as a merchant oligarchy: private interests coordinating through secret society rather than one king. Its tools are banking, navy, and diplomacy. Banking is the key because it extracts energy across time and space.

marks.text: extracts energy across time and space

paragraphs.text: The power rises as religion changes. Protestantism breaks Catholic hierarchy by offering direct access, egalitarianism, and faith, but each cure creates a new disease: confusion, sectarian chaos, and inward anxiety over whether belief is real. Calvin radicalizes the anxiety. If wealth proves God favors you and sin forbids spending, then the religious answer is endless work and investment. The bank loves this theology.

marks.text: The bank loves this theology

paragraphs.text: Then the Enlightenment removes active God and leaves a vacant throne. Money takes it. The Dutch move capital to England through William of Orange and the Bank of England, a private bank guaranteed by Parliament. The private banker keeps profit; the people guarantee loss. The same structure later appears as the Federal Reserve. Venice supplies the prototype: a poor, hard-to-invade trading city that becomes a parasite on empires.

marks.text: The private banker keeps profit; the people guarantee loss
a poor, hard-to-invade trading city that becomes a parasite on empires

beats.heading: Empire Is Financed More Than Fought
beats.summary: The Dutch Republic feeds on Spanish wealth, the Dutch East India Company becomes a corporate state, Jesuits and Freemasons fight a secret religious war, and England turns public debt into empire.

paragraphs.text: The Dutch Republic is energetic because Calvinism makes wealth a salvation signal. Spain has South American gold, and that wealth makes it lazy in Jiang's model. Dutch, British, and French industry feed on Spanish demand; Dutch piracy intercepts Spanish gold; the Dutch East India Company becomes a corporation almost like a nation unto itself, with army, currency, government, law, and monopoly.

marks.text: a corporation almost like a nation unto itself

paragraphs.text: The Reformation becomes a secret war. The Catholic side organizes through the Jesuits, the Protestant side through the Freemasons, and England becomes a central battlefield. The techniques are schools, infiltration, oaths, secret identity, and the same traumatic training already described earlier. Public doctrine and hidden intelligence are no longer separate stories.

marks.text: Public doctrine and hidden intelligence are no longer separate stories

paragraphs.text: The Bank of England changes war because Parliament makes debt durable. A king can die, default, lose, or be overthrown. A nation can be made to keep paying. That is how Britain can finance wars against Napoleon again and again. England does not have to win every battle. It is not mainly fighting; it is financing.

marks.text: It is not mainly fighting; it is financing

beats.heading: America Inherits The Machine
beats.summary: Britain extracts India, co-opts local elites through offshore storage, finances American monopolists, and leaves America to run the same system through private banking, technology fronts, dollar hegemony, symbolism, and renewed destruction.

paragraphs.text: British empire becomes a model of elite co-optation. India is turned into a resource colony: manufacturing destroyed, agriculture imposed, British goods purchased, local elites given a way to store wealth safely in England. The flag can leave and the system can remain, because offshore banking aligns local elites with the empire against their own people.

marks.text: The flag can leave and the system can remain

paragraphs.text: America receives the same machine under a national myth. Vanderbilt, Carnegie, Rockefeller, and Morgan are not just rags-to-riches heroes in this reading; they are local agents for capital that cannot openly appear as British after 1776. Modern technology repeats the trick. Military technology becomes Google or Facebook, and surveillance becomes voluntary because it arrives as a friendly platform rather than a Pentagon device.

marks.text: surveillance becomes voluntary because it arrives as a friendly platform

paragraphs.text: The dollar sequence completes the argument. Bretton Woods promises gold, Nixon breaks gold, oil requires dollars, and then Chinese labor becomes the hidden backing: millions working for useless paper. The lecture ends by reading America as Freemason symbolism and by predicting the same cycle again. Too much wealth makes people complacent; the system destroys the economy, starts war, creates anxiety, and forces focus back into work.

marks.text: Chinese labor becomes the hidden backing
creates anxiety, and forces focus back into work

questions.question: Why would a traumatized woman not remember who killed her family and hate the murderer rather than rely on him?
questions.answer: Jiang answers with dissociation: in trauma, the mind leaves because remembering the event in full would collapse the system. That is why the handler can be forgotten consciously while remaining able to command obedience later.

source_note: This read preserves Jiang's dated December 9, 2025 lecture argument, including controversial claims about secret societies, Satan or Saturn rituals, trauma programming, Jesuits, Freemasons, British finance, American monopolists, technology firms, U.S. currency, and World War III. It presents those claims as the source's internal model, not as independent historical, psychological, or political verification. Several spans contain ASR noise: 'secret societies' is sometimes rendered as 'six societies,' hashish as 'hashes,' Loyola as 'L Oreal,' Venice's marshes as 'Marcians,' and the next class topic as an uncertain phrase. The read follows the clear argumentative movement without reproducing those damaged forms.
